trajectory.description: Observation: Eval mae_60min=4.49 vs prior 4.45 baseline. Validation plateaus at val60=3.79 (epoch ~10) yet eval is much worse (4.49). Analysis: Big train/val gap suggests overfitting. Also: train.py uses TOD feature but predict.py passes zeros for TOD at test time — major distribution shift. Removing TOD entirely should help test consistency. Also there may be a stride issue: at test time we receive 6832 windows from a hidden test set; we need consistency. Let me retrain WITHOUT TOD feature (in_dim=1) to eliminate train/test mismatch, and possibly use a slightly smaller model with more regularization. Decision: Retrain with USE_TOD=0, longer with cosine schedule.

trajectory.command_output_summary:
f forward(self, x, supports):
        # x: [B, in_dim, N, T]
        in_len = x.size(3)
        if in_len < self.receptive_field:
            x = F.pad(x, (self.receptive_field - in_len, 0, 0, 0))
        adp = F.softmax(F.relu(torch.mm(self.nodevec1, self.nodevec2)), dim=1)
        new_supports = supports + [adp]

        x = self.start_conv(x)
        skip = 0
        for i in range(self.blocks * self.layers):
            residual = x
            f = torch.tanh(self.filter_convs[i](residual))
